attempts.prompt:
You are helping a robotic planning task. 
    Given the image of a scene and an instruction, generate the PDDL file with objects, initial state, and goal specification.
    Your output should adhere to the constraints defined in the domain file.
    You must output the PDDL file in the correct format.
    Examples of PDDL problems given a different domain instruction:
    Domain: hanoi
    Instruction: Move all disks to the rightmost peg.
    PDDL problem:
    (define (problem hanoi3)
        (:domain hanoi)
        (:objects
            peg1
            peg2
            orange_disk1
            orange_disk2
        )
            (:init
                (clear orange_disk1)
                (on orange_disk1 orange_disk2)
                (on orange_disk2 peg1)
            )
        (:goal (and (on orange_disk2 peg2) (on orange_disk1 orange_disk2)))
    )
    
    For the current domain, this is the domain file:
    (define (domain put_task)
 (:requirements
  :adl
 )
 (:types
  agent
  microwave fridge sink - receptacle  ; an object is a receptacle if it can hold things
  knife receptacle - object           ; if not an receptacle, default to object
  )


 (:predicates
    (openable ?r - receptacle)                                ; true if a receptacle is openable
    (opened ?r - receptacle)                                  ; true if a receptacle is opened
    (inReceptacle ?o - object ?r - object)                ; object ?o is in receptacle ?r
    (holds ?a - agent ?o - object)                            ; object ?o is held by agent ?a
    (holdsAny ?a - agent)                                     ; agent ?a holds an object
    (isClean ?o - object)                                     ; true if the object has been clean in sink
    (isHot ?o - object)                                       ; true if the object has been heated up
    (isCool ?o - object)                                      ; true if the object has been cooled
    (isOn ?o - object)                                        ; true if the object is on
    (isToggled ?o - object)                                   ; true if the object has been toggled
    (isSliced ?o - object)                                    ; true if the object is sliced
    (atLocation ?a - agent ?o - object)
 )

;; agent goes to receptacle
 (:action GotoLocation
    :parameters (?a - agent ?lEnd - object)
    :precondition (and
            (not (atLocation ?a ?lEnd))
            )
    :effect (and
                (atLocation ?a ?lEnd)
                (forall (?l - object)
                    (when (and (atLocation ?a ?l) (not (= ?l ?lEnd)))
                        (not (atLocation ?a ?l))))
                (forall (?l - object)
                     (when (inReceptacle ?l ?lEnd) (atLocation ?a ?l)))
            )
 )

;; agent opens receptacle
 (:action OpenObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?r - receptacle)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (openable ?r)
            )
    :effect (and
                (opened ?r)
            )
 )
;; agent closes receptacle
 (:action CloseObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?r - receptacle)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (openable ?r)
            (opened ?r)
            )
    :effect (and
                (not (opened ?r))
            )

 )

;; agent picks up object
 (:action PickupObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?o - object)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?o)
            (not (holdsAny ?a))
            )
    :effect (and
                (holds ?a ?o)
                (holdsAny ?a)
                (forall (?r - receptacle)
                    (not (inReceptacle ?o ?r))
                )
            )
 )

;; agent puts down an object in a receptacle
 (:action PutObjectInReceptacle
    :parameters (?a - agent ?o - object ?r - receptacle)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (holds ?a ?o)
            )
    :effect (and
                (inReceptacle ?o ?r)
                (not (holds ?a ?o))
                (not (holdsAny ?a))
            )
 )

;; agent cleans some object
 (:action CleanObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?r - sink ?o - object)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (holds ?a ?o)
            )
    :effect (and
                (isClean ?o)
            )
 )


;; agent heats-up some object
 (:action HeatObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?r - microwave ?o - object)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (holds ?a ?o)
            )
    :effect (and
                (isHot ?o)
            )
 )

;; agent cools some object
 (:action CoolObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?r - fridge ?o - object)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (holds ?a ?o)
            )
    :effect (and
                (isCool ?o)
            )
 )


;; agent toggle object
 (:action ToggleObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?o - object)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?o)
            )
    :effect (and
                (when (isOn ?o)
                    (not (isOn ?o)))
                (when (not (isOn ?o))
                    (isOn ?o))
                (isToggled ?o)
            )
 )


;; agent slices some object with a knife
 (:action SliceObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?co - object ?ko - knife)
    :precondition
            (and
                (atLocation ?a ?co)
                (holds ?a ?ko)
            )
    :effect (and
                (isSliced ?co)
            )
 )


)
    
        The image of the scene has been provided.
        Please first analyze the image and then generate the PDDL problem.
        
    The robot is initially not at any object's location. It needs to first go to a location to interact with an object. If an object A can hold another object B, it is a receptacle and must be recognized as such.
    Instruction: Put a chilled plate on the table.
    

SCENE CAPTION:
Scene Description:

The scene contains a kitchen environment with a central table and a dark-colored refrigerator visible in the background. The floor is composed of light-colored tiles adjacent to a wooden floor area.

Receptacles:
- There is one refrigerator, which is a dark, tall, rectangular appliance with a visible handle on its left side. It is positioned to the left of the table and appears to be closed. The refrigerator is an openable receptacle.
- The table itself is a two-tiered structure with a light gray top surface and a lower shelf. While it holds objects, it is not defined as a receptacle in the domain file and thus is treated as a static surface for object placement.

Objects (non-receptacles):
- A loaf of bread, brown in color, is placed on the table. It is a solid, rectangular object.
- A red tomato is positioned on the table to the right of the bread.
- A green lettuce head is on the table, to the right of the tomato.
- A silver knife is lying on the table to the left of the bread.
- A silver fork is placed on the table to the right of the bread and to the left of the tomato.
- A spoon is visible on the far left side of the table.
- Two small shakers (likely salt and pepper) are standing on the table, between the bread and the fork.

Agent State:
- The robot agent is not initially at any object’s location and must first navigate to an object to begin interaction.

Current State Relevant to Task:
- The refrigerator is closed (not opened).
- No objects are currently held by the agent (holdsAny is false).
- No objects are marked as clean, hot, cool, sliced, or toggled.
- The table is the target location for placing the chilled plate, but no plate is currently visible in the scene.

Spatial Relationships:
- All food items and utensils are arranged on the top surface of the table.
- The refrigerator is positioned to the left of the table and is not currently open.
- The agent must first go to the refrigerator to retrieve a plate (implied by the task, though no plate is visually present in the images), open it, retrieve the plate, cool it, and then place it on the table.

Note: Although the instruction refers to “a chilled plate,” no plate is visually present in the provided images. It is assumed that a plate exists inside the refrigerator and must be retrieved by the agent as part of the task. The scene does not show any plate, so its initial state (e.g., whether it is clean or not) cannot be determined from the images alone. The robot must first locate and retrieve the plate from the refrigerator before proceeding with cooling and placement.

Based on the above scene caption, the images, and the instruction, generate the PDDL problem file.
Use the caption to understand what objects are present and their current state.
Make sure the PDDL problem accurately reflects the scene described in the caption.

Generate the PDDL problem:
attempts.response:
```pddl
(define (problem put_chilled_plate_on_table)
    (:domain put_task)
    (:objects
        robot - agent
        fridge - fridge
        table - object
        plate - object
        bread - object
        tomato - object
        lettuce - object
        knife - knife
        fork - object
        spoon - object
        salt_shaker - object
        pepper_shaker - object
    )
    (:init
        (openable fridge)
        (not (opened fridge))
        (not (holdsAny robot))
        (not (isCool plate))
        (inReceptacle plate fridge)
        (atLocation robot table) ; assuming robot starts near table for initial navigation
    )
    (:goal
        (and
            (inReceptacle plate table)
            (isCool plate)
        )
    )
)
```
attempts.problem:
(define (problem put_chilled_plate_on_table)
    (:domain put_task)
    (:objects
        robot - agent
        fridge - fridge
        table - object
        plate - object
        bread - object
        tomato - object
        lettuce - object
        knife - knife
        fork - object
        spoon - object
        salt_shaker - object
        pepper_shaker - object
    )
    (:init
        (openable fridge)
        (not (opened fridge))
        (not (holdsAny robot))
        (not (isCool plate))
        (inReceptacle plate fridge)
        (atLocation robot table) ; assuming robot starts near table for initial navigation
    )
    (:goal
        (and
            (inReceptacle plate table)
            (isCool plate)
        )
    )
)

The scene contains a light gray, two-tiered table with white legs, positioned on a wooden floor against a plain, light beige wall. On the tabletop, several objects are arranged:

- A brown, rectangular loaf of bread is placed near the center-left.
- A red, round tomato is situated to the right of the bread.
- A green, leafy head of lettuce is positioned at the far right of the table.
- Two small, clear glass shakers with silver tops (likely salt and pepper) are located between the bread and the tomato.

- A silver knife is positioned to the left of the bread.
- A silver spoon is on the far left edge of the table.

In the second image, a large, dark brown, rectangular object is shown from a top-down perspective, partially obscuring the view. This object appears to be a fridge, with a visible metallic handle on its left side. Below it, a section of white tiled floor with gray veining is visible, adjacent to the wooden floor. The fridge is positioned to the left of the table.

The third image is a duplicate of the first, showing the same arrangement of objects on the table.

Relevant to the domain:
- The fridge is a receptacle and is openable. Its current state is not specified, but it is implied to be closed in the first and third images. In the second image, it appears to be open or being interacted with.
- The table is not defined as a receptacle in the domain, so it is treated as a standard object.
- The knife is an object and can be used for slicing.
- The bread, tomato, and lettuce are objects that can be sliced or manipulated.

final.prompt:
You are helping a robotic planning task. 
    Given the image of a scene and an instruction, generate the PDDL file with objects, initial state, and goal specification.
    Your output should adhere to the constraints defined in the domain file.
    You must output the PDDL file in the correct format.
    Examples of PDDL problems given a different domain instruction:
    Domain: hanoi
    Instruction: Move all disks to the rightmost peg.
    PDDL problem:
    (define (problem hanoi3)
        (:domain hanoi)
        (:objects
            peg1
            peg2
            orange_disk1
            orange_disk2
        )
            (:init
                (clear orange_disk1)
                (on orange_disk1 orange_disk2)
                (on orange_disk2 peg1)
            )
        (:goal (and (on orange_disk2 peg2) (on orange_disk1 orange_disk2)))
    )
    
    For the current domain, this is the domain file:
    (define (domain put_task)
 (:requirements
  :adl
 )
 (:types
  agent
  microwave fridge sink - receptacle  ; an object is a receptacle if it can hold things
  knife receptacle - object           ; if not an receptacle, default to object
  )


 (:predicates
    (openable ?r - receptacle)                                ; true if a receptacle is openable
    (opened ?r - receptacle)                                  ; true if a receptacle is opened
    (inReceptacle ?o - object ?r - object)                ; object ?o is in receptacle ?r
    (holds ?a - agent ?o - object)                            ; object ?o is held by agent ?a
    (holdsAny ?a - agent)                                     ; agent ?a holds an object
    (isClean ?o - object)                                     ; true if the object has been clean in sink
    (isHot ?o - object)                                       ; true if the object has been heated up
    (isCool ?o - object)                                      ; true if the object has been cooled
    (isOn ?o - object)                                        ; true if the object is on
    (isToggled ?o - object)                                   ; true if the object has been toggled
    (isSliced ?o - object)                                    ; true if the object is sliced
    (atLocation ?a - agent ?o - object)
 )

;; agent goes to receptacle
 (:action GotoLocation
    :parameters (?a - agent ?lEnd - object)
    :precondition (and
            (not (atLocation ?a ?lEnd))
            )
    :effect (and
                (atLocation ?a ?lEnd)
                (forall (?l - object)
                    (when (and (atLocation ?a ?l) (not (= ?l ?lEnd)))
                        (not (atLocation ?a ?l))))
                (forall (?l - object)
                     (when (inReceptacle ?l ?lEnd) (atLocation ?a ?l)))
            )
 )

;; agent opens receptacle
 (:action OpenObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?r - receptacle)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (openable ?r)
            )
    :effect (and
                (opened ?r)
            )
 )
;; agent closes receptacle
 (:action CloseObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?r - receptacle)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (openable ?r)
            (opened ?r)
            )
    :effect (and
                (not (opened ?r))
            )

 )

;; agent picks up object
 (:action PickupObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?o - object)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?o)
            (not (holdsAny ?a))
            )
    :effect (and
                (holds ?a ?o)
                (holdsAny ?a)
                (forall (?r - receptacle)
                    (not (inReceptacle ?o ?r))
                )
            )
 )

;; agent puts down an object in a receptacle
 (:action PutObjectInReceptacle
    :parameters (?a - agent ?o - object ?r - receptacle)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (holds ?a ?o)
            )
    :effect (and
                (inReceptacle ?o ?r)
                (not (holds ?a ?o))
                (not (holdsAny ?a))
            )
 )

;; agent cleans some object
 (:action CleanObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?r - sink ?o - object)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (holds ?a ?o)
            )
    :effect (and
                (isClean ?o)
            )
 )


;; agent heats-up some object
 (:action HeatObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?r - microwave ?o - object)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (holds ?a ?o)
            )
    :effect (and
                (isHot ?o)
            )
 )

;; agent cools some object
 (:action CoolObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?r - fridge ?o - object)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (holds ?a ?o)
            )
    :effect (and
                (isCool ?o)
            )
 )


;; agent toggle object
 (:action ToggleObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?o - object)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?o)
            )
    :effect (and
                (when (isOn ?o)
                    (not (isOn ?o)))
                (when (not (isOn ?o))
                    (isOn ?o))
                (isToggled ?o)
            )
 )


;; agent slices some object with a knife
 (:action SliceObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?co - object ?ko - knife)
    :precondition
            (and
                (atLocation ?a ?co)
                (holds ?a ?ko)
            )
    :effect (and
                (isSliced ?co)
            )
 )


)
